zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Lowe於1552年1月21日出生在英國德比夏爾丹比時，她的父親Mr. Lowe30歲，母親Mrs. Lowe27歲。她於1560年1月30日與Thomas Leyson在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾格亞特 霍頓結婚。她去世於1614年9月在英國諾薩普頓史爾素爾格拉福享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Lowe于1552年1月21日出生在英国德比夏爾丹比时，她的父亲Mr. Lowe30岁，母亲Mrs. Lowe27岁。她于1560年1月30日与Thomas Leyson在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾格亞特 霍頓结婚。她去世于1614年9月在英国諾薩普頓史爾素爾格拉福享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Lowe
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Low  .

German (Löwe) and Danish (Løwe): from Middle High German lēwe, löuwe ‘lion’, hence a nickname for a brave or regal person. In some cases the surname may have been a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn distinguished by the sign of a lion. It is also found in Sweden. Compare Loewe 1, Lau , Lave , and Lurvey .
Jewish (Ashkenazic; Löwe): artificial name from German Löwe ‘lion’.

variants.name: Lau
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wai, Wing, Kam, Kwok, Ming, Chi, Yuk, Chun, Kin, Ching, Kwan, Man.

German: nickname for a physically strong person, from Middle High German louwe, lauwe ‘lion’. In some cases the surname may have been a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn distinguished by the sign of a lion. Compare Lowe .
German: topographic name for someone living in a bush-covered area or clearing, Middle Low German lo, loch, lage, Middle High German lō.

variants.name: Loew
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Eckhard, Fritz, Hans, Heinz, Hermann, Manfred, Raimund.
German (mainly Löw): variant of Löwe ‘lion’ (see Lowe 2). The surname Loew is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Low 5.

variants.name: Lobe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Alois, Erwin, Horst, Otto, Winfried.
German (Löbe): variant of Löwe ‘lion’ (see Lowe 2).
German: metonymic occupational name for a tanner, from Middle High German lō ‘tanning bark’.

variants.name: Loewe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Armin, Gerhard, Helmuth, Klaus, Kurt.
German (mainly Löwe): see Lowe 2.
Jewish (Ashkenazic; also Löwe): artificial name from German Löwe ‘lion’. Compare Lowe 3.

variants.name: Loewen
meanings: German (Löwen): variant of Löwe ‘lion’ (see Lowe 2). Compare Lowen 2.

variants.name: Low
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name for someone who lived near a tumulus, mound or hill, Middle English lowe, from Old English hlāw (see Law 2).

Scottish and English: nickname for a short man, from Middle English lah, lowe (Old Norse lágr; the word was adopted first into the northern dialects of Middle English, where Scandinavian influence was strong, and then spread south, with regular alteration of the vowel quality).

English and Scottish (of Norman origin): nickname for a violent or dangerous person, from Anglo-Norman French lou, leu ‘wolf’ (from Latin lupus). Wolves were relatively common in Britain at the time when most surnames were formed, as there still existed large tracts of uncleared forest.

variants.name: Lurvey
meanings: Americanized form of German Löwe ‘lion’ (see Lowe 2).
History: Peter Lurvy, the immigrant ancestor of this family, was in Ipswich, MA, by 1679.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Lowe was born on 21 January 1552, in Denby, Derbyshire, England, her father, Mr. Lowe, was 30 and her mother, Mrs. Lowe, was 27. She married Thomas Leyson on 30 January 1560, in Great Houghton, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom. She died in September 1614, in Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, England, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Lowe est née le 21 janvier 1552, à Denby, Derbyshire, Angleterre, son père, Mr. Lowe, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Mrs. Lowe, avait 27 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Leyson le 30 janvier 1560, à Great Houghton, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Elle est décédée en septembre 1614, à Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Lowe am 21. Januar 1552 in Denby, Derbyshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Mr. Lowe 30 und ihre Mutter Mrs. Lowe 27. Sie hat am 30. Januar 1560 in Great Houghton, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom Thomas Leyson geheiratet. Sie starb im September 1614 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Lowe è nata il il 21 gennaio 1552 nel Denby, Derbyshire, England, sua padre, Mr. Lowe, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Mrs. Lowe, aveva 27 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Leyson il 30 gennaio 1560, nel Great Houghton, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì nel settembre 1614, nel Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, England, all'età di 62 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Loweが1552年1月21日にイギリスデルブイシュアイアデンビーで生まれた時，父Mr. Loweは30歳，母Mrs. Loweは27歳であった。1560年1月30日にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアグレアト ホーウトンでThomas Leysonと結婚した。彼女は1614年9月にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアスルグラブで62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Lowe(이)가 1552년 1월 21일 영국 더비실르 덴비에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Mr. Lowe(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. Lowe(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1560년 1월 30일 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 그리트 허그h턴에서 Thomas Leyson(와)과 결혼했다. 그녀는 1614년 9월 영국 노르섬프토실르 설그레이브에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Lowe nasceu em 21 de janeiro de 1552, em Denby, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Mr. Lowe, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. Lowe, tinha 27 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Leyson em 30 de janeiro de 1560, em Great Houghton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ela faleceu em setembro de 1614, em Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, com 62 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Lowe родилась 21 января 1552 г. в местности денби, дирбишир, Англия, её отцу по имени Mr. Lowe было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. Lowe, 27. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Leyson 30 января 1560 г. в местности грит хоугтон, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания. Она умерла сентябрь 1614 г. в местности сулграв, нортамптоншир, Англия в возрасте 62 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Lowe nació el 21 de enero de 1552, en Denby, Derbyshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Mr. Lowe, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Mrs. Lowe, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Thomas Leyson el 30 de enero de 1560, en Great Houghton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió en septiembre de 1614, en Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 62 años.
